human_verification.notes: Reviewed hospital HIM specialty boundaries, planning and allocation outputs, healthcare source authority mapping, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisite handling, PHI and minimum-necessary controls, clinical/payer/coding/privacy escalation rules, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required domain research scenario families are mapped to explicit scenario IDs.
All shared, planning-scheduling-and-allocation, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are validated and covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package.
No unreviewed regressions are present in this package build.

specialist_owned_evidence: Authority boundaries are explicit for HIM queue planning versus staffing, budget, release, amendment, coding, payer-policy, claim, denial, clinical, privacy, legal, finance, compliance, and final operational approval.
Research sources cover public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

Scenario map covers normal in-scope HIM execution, capacity shortfall, priority conflict, final approval refusal, authorization/referral/eligibility/documentation blockers, PHI handling, clinical safety escalation, coding and medical necessity boundaries, low confidence, source conflict, terminology handling, tenant adaptation, and payer/privacy interpretation escalation.
Commercialization and deployment artifacts define specification-based operation and separate retained-implementation promotion criteria.
materialization_requirements: Tenant task metadata: queue type, work item ID, patient/member/encounter/account IDs, requested action, required output, priority request, due date, and source-system references.

Boundary metadata: allowed decision types, adjacent owners, refusal triggers, approval matrix, privacy owner, clinical owner, coding owner, payer owner, legal/compliance owner, and finance/operations approver.

Tenant-supplied or retrieved assumptions: jurisdiction, facility, business unit, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, coverage rules, patient/member status, service dates, authorization status, referral dependencies, current systems of record, retrieval hooks, role-based access, source precedence, record locations, privacy restrictions, and minimum-necessary scope.

Regulated-lane caveats: HIPAA, CMS, hospital Conditions of Participation, payer/provider operating rules, tenant policy, state Medicaid, state medical-record law, legal hold, and clinical safety boundaries may narrow the public baseline.

Refusal or orchestrator-return conditions: missing tenant facts, missing authoritative source context, missing records, source conflicts, PHI uncertainty, clinical risk, payer-policy interpretation, coding decision, medical necessity, privacy/legal interpretation, financial approval, staffing approval, final operational approval, or unsupported disclosure.
notes: The pack is safe for specification-based runtime and does not claim trusted built authority.
Promotion to retained implementation is deferred to a separate explicit build, tenant integration, and approval event.
